cycleContext.note: June 8 scan (14-day window: 2026-05-25–2026-06-08). Key signals: (1) Bolivia military crackdown law signed June 8 — 10 killed, 365+ arrested; (2) Ebola DRC 515/91 + Uganda 19/2, 8 in Kampala, 27 new cases June 5-6; (3) Iran 784 executions YTD, 126th week hunger strike; (4) Oracle June 15 WARN Act sign-or-forfeit deadline; (5) ICE June 4 ended death-reporting for released detainees; (6) India ~5,000 Bengali Muslims expelled; (7) UAE-Sudan RSF genocide arms documentation June 8; (8) Microsoft Azure-Israel inquiry findings June 8 — violations found, Unit 8200 access terminated, new Human Rights Review Board; (9) Norway SWF backed Alphabet governance proposal re: Project Nimbus; (10) Great American AI Act bipartisan discussion draft released June 4; (11) Haiti record displacement 1.5M; (12) Yemen 73 UN staff held by Houthis 2-year mark; (13) Australia $1.43B PFAS suit vs 3M filed May 28.

newMaterialEventsInWindow: June 8: Bolivia military crackdown law signed — 10 killed, 365+ arrested, La Paz/El Alto blockaded
June 8: UAE arming Sudan RSF genocide — Refugees International + Conflict Insights Group + HRW new investigations
June 8: Microsoft Azure-Israel inquiry published — Unit 8200 surveillance of Palestinian calls confirmed, access terminated, Human Rights Review Board created
June 5-6: 27 new Ebola cases in DRC in 24 hrs; Uganda 8 cases now in Kampala
June 7: HRW + Amnesty + UNSC: Yemen Houthis should release 73 detained UN/civil society staff on 2nd anniversary
June 6: Haiti record displacement — Port-au-Prince IDP count exceeds 300,000 for first time
June 4: Great American AI Act bipartisan discussion draft released — mandatory audits for frontier labs
June 4: Norway sovereign wealth fund backed Alphabet Project Nimbus oversight proposal
June 4: ICE Acting Director memo ended post-release detainee death reporting
June 2: Trump signs voluntary AI safety EO — 30-day pre-release model review
May 28: Australia filed $1.43B PFAS lawsuit against 3M — largest ever by Australia

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.summary: 784 documented executions in 2026 YTD per Iran Human Rights Society. "No to Executions Tuesdays" campaign entered its 126th consecutive week (June 9) across 56 prisons. 18 prisoners hanged May 31–June 1 alone; Kurdish political prisoners Ashkan Maleki and Mehrdad Mohammadinia executed in secret June 1. Expedited death-penalty trials for 2026 protesters — arrest to execution in months. Internet censorship masking true scale.

top_entities.name: Bolivia
top_entities.summary: June 7: Chamber of Deputies passed law authorising military deployment against protesters with presumption-of-legality shield. June 8: President Paz signed. As of June 8: 10 killed, 37 injured, 365+ arrested. La Paz/El Alto blockaded entering 7th week. Terrorism charges against 25 union leaders. Three deaths from blocked hospital supplies; one in direct police clash. Worst economic crisis in four decades.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
top_entities.summary: Ebola Bundibugyo: as of June 6 DRC 515 confirmed cases / 91 deaths; 134 new confirmed cases and 27 new deaths in 24 hrs June 5–6. 17 of 36 health zones in Ituri affected. Travel restrictions reimposed around Bunia. WHO PHEIC active. No approved vaccine for Bundibugyo strain. Uganda 19 cases / 2 deaths — 8 in Kampala (all linked to DRC travellers). Concurrent 26.5M acutely hungry; DRC on most-neglected displacement list 10th year running.

top_entities.name: Uganda
top_entities.summary: Ebola Bundibugyo cross-border spread: 19 confirmed cases / 2 deaths as of June 6. Eight cases in Kampala, one in Wakiso. All traceable to DRC travellers — no documented community transmission yet. WHO and CDC monitoring closely given urban exposure risk in Kampala (pop. 3M+).

top_entities.name: Oracle Corporation
top_entities.summary: June 15 WARN Act severance-release deadline is live. ~30,000 workers face sign-or-forfeit ultimatum. WARN 60-day pay consumed entire severance pool for many workers, leaving zero incremental benefit. RSUs forfeited without acceleration for all affected. Vulnerable groups include H-1B holders facing deportation, cancer patients, pregnant workers, veterans, near-vesting employees. State investigations in WA and MO. Coworker.org petition active.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.summary: June 1–2: largest combined bombardment of 2026 — 656 drones + 73 cruise missiles — killed 22 civilians including 3-year-old and 8-year-old in Dnipro; 138 wounded. June 4: 12 more killed in follow-up strikes. Putin rejected direct peace talks June 6. UN verified 60,659 civilian casualties (15,850 killed) since Feb 2022. Ukraine air defence shortage worsening civilian exposure; civilian terror-strike strategy accelerating as battlefield gains stall.

top_entities.name: United States
top_entities.summary: June 4: ICE Acting Director memo ended mandatory public reporting of detainee deaths within 30 days of release. 18 detainee deaths Jan–May 2026 (one every 6 days — highest rate in 20+ years); total detained pop. 60,000–70,000. Policy eliminates oversight of whether detention conditions caused post-release deaths. ACLU: structural accountability rollback. Concurrent: EEOC sent Title VII DEI-compliance warning to all Fortune 500 CEOs.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.summary: DOJ criminal and civil Medicare Advantage fraud probe active (inflated risk scores, unconfirmed physician diagnoses). Company launched third-party review of billing practices. Arizona AG June 1 named UnitedHealthcare in 8-insurer price-fixing cartel suit (MultiPlan algorithm). Grand jury subpoena to MultiPlan confirmed in May 18 SEC filing. Lifepoint hospital system filed separate federal complaint June 2. Elevance Health CMS sanctions threat for risk-adjustment noncompliance adds sector breadth.

top_entities.name: India
top_entities.summary: HRW: 1,500+ Bengali-speaking Muslims expelled to Bangladesh May 7 – June 15 without citizenship verification; ~5,000 total deported. 836 still in detention. Arbitrary round-ups of low-income migrant workers in BJP-governed states without due process. West Bengal CM acknowledged scale. Political motivation alleged ahead of elections. IHL/domestic law violations documented. Ethiopia election-restriction parallel emerging.

top_entities.name: Microsoft
top_entities.summary: June 8: Microsoft published findings of internal Azure-Israel investigation. Found Unit 8200 used Azure to collect, play back, and analyse content of millions of Palestinian phone calls daily — violating ToS. Access terminated. New Human Rights Review Board announced. Head of Microsoft Israel Aaron Haimovich resigned May 31 amid ethics code violation probe. Microsoft still provides other services (Azure AI, translation) to Israeli Ministry of Defence. Norway SWF backed Alphabet governance proposal on same issue June 4.

top_entities.name: Alphabet (Google)
top_entities.summary: June 4: Norway sovereign wealth fund (world's largest, $2T) backed shareholder proposal for independent oversight of Project Nimbus cloud/AI contract with Israeli military — governance escalation. Leaked internal docs show Google knew pre-contract it could not control how Israeli military would use ML tools. Florida filed landmark lawsuit against OpenAI/Google alleging unsafe addictive AI technology causing harms to children. Great American AI Act would require twice-yearly independent audits of frontier labs including Google.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.summary: June 4: Great American AI Act bipartisan draft bill introduced — would require frontier labs (>$500M revenue) to publish safety frameworks and undergo twice-yearly independent audits. OpenAI released Frontier Governance Framework May 28, aligning internally with EU AI Act and CA Transparency Act. June 3: OpenAI diverged from White House on oversight locus (wants CAISI/civilian, not NSA). Florida filed lawsuit against OpenAI for allegedly knowingly releasing addictive unsafe ChatGPT causing harms to children. June 2: Trump EO on voluntary AI safety pre-release review.

top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.summary: Great American AI Act (introduced June 4) would impose mandatory twice-yearly safety audits on Anthropic as a frontier lab. Trump's June 2 EO on voluntary AI pre-release review includes Anthropic. Claude Mythos Preview remains gated — only ~50 partner orgs in Project Glasswing due to dual-use concern. RSP v3 downgrade and Pentagon dispute DC Circuit appeal ongoing. Norway SWF using Claude AI for ESG screening (positive signal). OpenAI EU cyber-model access contrasts with Anthropic still withholding Mythos in EU.

top_entities.name: 3M
top_entities.summary: May 28: Australia filed A$2B ($1.43B) PFAS lawsuit against 3M — largest ever brought by Australia — for contamination at 28 military bases. 3M knew environmental risks and allegedly withheld/misrepresented them to Australian government. A$1.3B already spent on remediation including A$408M in community settlements; 200,000+ metric tons contaminated soil removed; 13B+ litres water treated. Phase 2 filing deadline July 31. Minnesota new PFAS suit also active.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.summary: June 5 OCHA: 45 Palestinians killed, 254 injured May 20–June 3 in Gaza. June 6: at least 10 killed in Israeli strikes including drone attack on tent camp for displaced. Post-ceasefire (Oct 2025) death toll passed 970 fatalities. Cairo talks beginning June 9. Israeli military conducting ongoing operations in West Bank: 50 arrested, homes demolished, Birzeit University assaulted. UN OHCHR: ethnic cleansing concerns. Microsoft Azure-Israel inquiry found violations, terminated Unit 8200 access.

sector_alerts.title: Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC — DRC/Uganda (sector: global health)
sector_alerts.summary: 515 DRC cases / 91 deaths; 19 Uganda cases including 8 in Kampala as of June 6. 27 new cases in 24 hrs (June 5–6). No vaccine. WHO PHEIC active. Affects: Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, and any entity with humanitarian-health programmes in Central Africa.

sector_alerts.title: Great American AI Act discussion draft (sector: AI governance)
sector_alerts.summary: June 4 bipartisan draft bill requires frontier labs (>$500M revenue) to publish safety frameworks and submit to twice-yearly independent audits. Includes 3-year preemption of state AI laws. Affects: OpenAI, Google/Alphabet, Anthropic, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, xAI.

sector_alerts.title: UAE documented arming RSF in Sudan genocide (sector: arms / sovereignty)
sector_alerts.summary: Multiple June 8 investigations confirm UAE continued weapons supply to RSF via Libya/Chad/Uganda. Colombian mercenaries trained on UAE bases. UN Feb 2026 genocide determination. 225,000 dead, 14M displaced, 5M in famine. Affects: UAE, Sudan, and arms-supply chain states.

sector_alerts.title: ICE death-reporting rollback — US detention accountability (sector: immigration / governance)
sector_alerts.summary: June 4 memo ends 30-day post-release death reporting. 18 detainee deaths in 5 months of 2026 — highest rate in 20+ years. Pop. 60,000–70,000. Accountability gap now structural. Affects: United States.

sector_alerts.title: Trump voluntary AI safety EO (sector: AI governance)
sector_alerts.summary: June 2 executive order asks companies to voluntarily submit frontier models for 30-day pre-release government review. No mandatory framework. Affects: all frontier AI labs.

sector_alerts.title: Health insurance sector DOJ / state AG actions (sector: healthcare)
sector_alerts.summary: Arizona AG June 1 named 8 insurers including UnitedHealth in MultiPlan price-fixing cartel. DOJ grand jury subpoena confirmed. Elevance Health CMS sanctions threat. Affects: UnitedHealth Group, Elevance Health, Cigna, Aetna/CVS Health.

sector_alerts.title: Yemen: Houthis holding 73 UN/civil-society workers — 2-year mark (sector: humanitarian)
sector_alerts.summary: June 6–7 anniversary appeals from HRW, Amnesty, UNSC. One WFP worker died in custody. No lawyer access. Aid disruption worsening food crisis.

sector_alerts.title: Fortune 500 EEOC DEI Title VII warning (sector: corporate governance / equity)
sector_alerts.summary: EEOC chair issued compliance reminder to all Fortune 500 CEOs re Title VII obligations related to DEI programmes. ILO gig-worker protection treaty negotiations in June 2026 also add labour-rights pressure on platform companies.

HRW April 2026: persecution of Tigrayans unrelenting.

entity_reviews.name: Democratic Republic of C
entity_reviews.summary: Ebola Bundibugyo: as of June 6 DRC 515 confirmed cases / 91 deaths; 134 new confirmed cases and 27 new deaths in 24 hrs June 5–6. 17 of 36 health zones in Ituri affected. Travel restrictions reimposed around Bunia. WHO PHEIC active. No approved vaccine for Bundibugyo strain. Uganda 19 cases / 2 deaths — 8 in Kampala (all linked to DRC travellers). Concurrent 26.5M acutely hungry; DRC on most-neglected displacement list 10th year running.

entity_reviews.name: Oracle Corporation
entity_reviews.summary: June 15 WARN Act severance-release deadline is live. ~30,000 workers face sign-or-forfeit ultimatum. WARN 60-day pay consumed entire severance pool for many workers, leaving zero incremental benefit. RSUs forfeited without acceleration for all affected. Vulnerable groups include H-1B holders facing deportation, cancer patients, pregnant workers, veterans, near-vesting employees. State investigations in WA and MO. Coworker.org petition active.
